message:
feat(string_abi,libc.map): route explicit_memset through ABI memset + add memset_explicit alias + export missing BSD/Linux stragglers

* string_abi.rs::explicit_memset — instead of calling host `libc::memset` directly, route through our own `memset` ABI entry. This gives the explicit-zeroing path the same membrane accounting, null-pointer guard (return null when n>0 and dest is null instead of crashing in host memset), and volatile byte-fill semantics that all other writes through our libc see. The `std::hint::black_box(s)` barrier is preserved so dead-store elimination still can't optimize the fill out, since `memset`'s return value is what we propagate now.

* string_abi.rs::memset_explicit — new C23 entry, defined as a thin alias of `explicit_memset`. NetBSD ships this as a synonym; C23 standardized the spelling.

* string_abi_test.rs:
  - `bd_jt6vm_explicit_memset_null_nonzero_uses_memset_null_guard` — pin the new behavior: `explicit_memset(NULL, 0xAB, 4)` must return NULL (the memset null guard), not crash. Previously this would have tried to memset host memory at address 0.
  - `bd_jt6vm_memset_explicit_alias_writes_byte_value` — confirm the C23 alias writes the pattern byte across the buffer and returns the same destination pointer.

* version_scripts/libc.map — export the symbols that already exist in source but were missing from the version script (so external tooling can resolve them via versioned dlsym):
  - `explicit_memset`, `memset_explicit` (this commit), `consttime_bcmp`, `consttime_memequal` (already in string_abi.rs since the early BSD-parity port) — added in the BSD/timing-safe block alongside `timingsafe_bcmp`/`timingsafe_memcmp`
  - `reallocarr` — already in stdlib_abi.rs, missing from the BSD-utility block
  - `pidfile_signal` — already in stdlib_abi.rs (now correctness-fixed in the previous commit), missing from the pidfile block
  - `futex_waitv`, `futex_wake`, `futex_wait`, `futex_requeue` — already in unistd_abi.rs, missing from the syscall-veneer block

These libc.map additions don't change runtime behavior — they just make the existing exports visible through versioned symbol lookup.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
